title: Jawline Injection Techniques | Injecting the Jaw Safely
filename: Jawline Injection Techniques ｜ Injecting the Jaw Safely.en.vtt
excerpt: What I'm interested in here is this lateral fat pad here which has got smaller and you can sometimes test this well on your patients if you pull back a little you can sometimes see the difference that volume makes so happens that it's part of the shape of your jawline and then as…

segments.text: what I'm interested in here is this lateral fat pad here which has got smaller and you can sometimes test this well on your patients if you pull back a little you can sometimes see the difference that volume makes so happens that it's part of the shape of your jawline and then as you get older you also tend to lose bone at the GoNoodle angle so this becomes a flatter shape rather than a more angled shape so you're able to pull the skin back and improve the shape of the bone underneath you can do this with a cannula but there's a really big downside to cannulas which is they tend to be in The Superficial fat so that's where they gravitate towards so when you open your mouth if you open your mouth for me now see how the jawline moves down here well the filler would stay up here open again and it stays put so that's not natural so I do prefer placing on the GoNoodle angle here because that'll move with the skin and I think that's basically what we're trying to emulate nature not just do

something that works only in one position now of course we have the facial artery to think about which thankfully you can often feel a pulse and you can feel the Notch and that's where we absolutely will never inject on the bone here that's where the most dangerous place you could inject and the whole face so instead um I will be lateral to that feeling for that shape here and placing it right on the peripheral side of that of the GoNoodle angle so just like we do with the cheek I like to feel for the angle I've got thumb and forefinger feeling for the shape of it I can see exactly where that and where that bone is engine there we go right on it now it's perfect it's not deep if your needle is going too deep you've missed it I'm going to put pressure underneath to stop the product flowing inferiorly so I really wanted to have

this shape that's great give it a second when I say a second I mean five seconds and then we inject there's often a second point you can do slightly higher up as well only slightly higher and that can give you a bit more lift I'm going to put a new needle on there

okay so chin up and look a little bit away from me so entry point I want to be able to reach both corners of a triangle I think of it sometimes like a little bow tie shape to replace the volume here I need to go up there and to the midline so that's my entry point so once you've made an entry point if your entry point doesn't bleed then you can't always find it again so a little bit of alcohol wipe often makes it stand out there it is a little bit of redness there's appeared that's my entry point simple trick to help you find it and then you're feeling for the entry of the hole like this just going around like a blind man in the dark feeling something gently pop into it you don't have to jab it until you find it to tickle in

and I'm just easing in trying to find an easy path there's no point you do see people just shove it through I quite like the idea that this candidates are meant to decrease trauma that we we're gentle find the right way there are quite a few little nerves around here got the mental nerve emerging from the foramen we could be close to that now I'm hitting a little obstacle no matter which way I go now so my next trick is to give a little squirt of product and that can open up the space a little I go right up against the thing that's blocking little squirt come back slightly and then try and find a way through and it often pops through there we go that's it Cannon is now right at the midline at the mental crease now this is not going to treat the mental crease because it's too deep but it's going to replace the fat pad

the fat pad that allows the crease to develop and once you're in you can paint with a cannula you can just slide in and out allow the product to flow anterior and retrograde injection integrating retrograde we should see there that's harmonizing already that's already a an easier transition so and we've done half of it so we've volumized to the midline now we're up to the oral Commission Now same process actually can help if you pretend like you're on the phone in the 80s put your if you were to slide your head in that direction and pinch that's it that's perfect uh sorry chin up slightly more there we go so it's a slightly awkward angle with a cannula but once you're in

it's fine it's just getting there edging up slowly aiming for the Oracle measure feeling with my supporting hand where I'm going so support the oral commissioner you can aspirate with Canada's I don't really think it's likely if you've been very gentle all the way in and the candle is quite mobile like I can see it is I don't think you're adding a lot but if you're in a tight space I recommend it and particularly noses otherwise we're just trickling the product in placing that fat pad supporting the oral commissioner
it's very sculptural so you can maybe animate afterwards what I'm doing but it's painting a little trickle all across that triangle we drew in the beginning July is looking straighter [Music] foreign
